Sport

Men's Basketball Roster Schedule & Results Coaching Staff Statistics - Season Additional Links

2025-26 Men's Basketball Roster

Stella Flahave

5 Chandler Meeks

  • Height 6-1
  • Weight 185
  • Class Redshirt Junior
  • Hometown Omaha, Neb.
  • Bio
  • Stats
  • Historical

Biography

Season Highs
Points: 164 (2022-23)
Field Goals Made: 59 (2022-23)
Free Throws Made: 35 (2022-23)
Three Pointers Made: 11 (2022-23)
Rebounds: 50 (2022-23)
Assists: 31 (2022-23)
Blocks: 10 (2022-23)
Steals: 16 (2024-25)

Game Highs
Points: 27 (vs. Southwest Minnesota State, January 28, 2023)
Field Goals Made: 9 (Two Times)
Free Throws Made: 7 (Three Times)
Three Pointers Made: 2 (Six Times)
Rebounds: 6 (Two Times)
Assists: 4 (Three Times)
Blocks: 2 (Four Times)
Steals: 3 (Four Times)

2025-26 (Redshirt Senior): Meeks only played in 3 games on the season because of injury. He had a total of 45 minutes on the court and went 6 for 19, scoring 18 points on the year. 

2024-25 (Redshirt Junior): Meeks Started in all 15 games he played. He picked up an injury on the 15th game of the season which knocked him out for the year. Meeks shot .439 from the field scoring 145 points on the season. He was 31 for 37 from the free-throw line. He wrapped up his season with 27 rebounds, 19 assists, 16 steals, and six blocks. 

2023-24 (Redshirt Sophomore): Missed entire season.

2022-23 (Freshman): Dynamic freshman season for the Golden Eagles...Had some injury issues in his first year, playing 21 games...Seven double-digit scoring games on the season...Career best 27 points on 9-16 shooting from the field and 7-9 from the free throw line in 36 minutes, all of which a career-high...Three games with three steals...73 percent free throw shooter.

Prior to UMC: Meeks played as a prep at Omaha Westside H.S. As a senior, he averaged 9.8 points, 3.5 rebounds, and 2.9 assists per game. As a junior, Meeks notched 12.5 points, 5.2 rebounds, and 3.5 assists per tilt. As a sophomore, he notched 9.7 points, 3.7 rebounds, and 1.5 assists per game. Meeks had a season-high 18 points in a win over Lincoln East H.S. March 2. He had 16 points in a victory over Grand Island H.S. February 12. Meeks had 17 points in a win over Millard North H.S. December 29. As a junior, Meeks notched 29 points in a win over Elkhorn South H.S. He had 22 points in a victory over Norfolk H.S. December 12. Meeks picked up 24 points in a victory over Bryan H.S. Meeks was named to the All-Metro Second Team as a senior for Omaha Westside H.S.

Personal: He is the son of Al Meeks, Jr. and Cherise Meeks. Meeks plans to major in business. He has been involved in DECA, and is a member of the honor roll. His brother Trae Meeks played at Avila University.
